title: Emergency handwashing station
summary: A low-water handwashing point for food, toilet, patient care, animal contact, and water-container handling.
content: When water is limited, handwashing often disappears exactly when infection risk rises. A station can be simple: a clean pour container, soap, catch basin, drying plan, and reminder card. Place it where people naturally pass after toilet use and before food handling.
steps: Place it between dirty and clean zones.
Tie or place soap so it cannot fall into wastewater.
Collect wastewater away from food and sleeping spaces.
Check soap and water twice daily.
warnings: Shared dirty towels can spread illness.
Hand sanitizer is not enough when hands are visibly dirty.
Do not let wastewater run toward food storage.
common_mistakes: Putting the station too far away while carrying out “Emergency handwashing station”
Using an open bucket everyone touches.
Forgetting handwashing before touching safe water.
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Emergency handwashing station with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Emergency handwashing station if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Emergency handwashing station and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Emergency handwashing station or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Emergency handwashing station are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Emergency handwashing station”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Emergency handwashing station as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Emergency handwashing station when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Emergency handwashing station.
short_term: For the first hours of Emergency handwashing station, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Emergency handwashing station remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Emergency handwashing station fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Emergency handwashing station to the real environment: check water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
